reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: Quirky take on visiting waterfalls. A bit gimmicky but different and cool. We found it a little difficult to figure out quite what was going on, got rushed onto coaster,a bit forceful moving legs into place taking no regards to a previous injury, fully accept this was our issue as we didn't speak sufficient vietnamese. Unclear whether you could walk to or from waterfall, from what I could ascertain the walking path was closed. Shared a cart on way down, the two carts in front kept stopping on the track so we had to stop but cart behind us didn't always stop, bumping into us, not hard but a bit stressful. Can't say I enjoyed the trip down as much as I'd expected. Waterfalls pleasent at bottom. Didn't stay long but had spotted a sign which gave a weight limit for carts of 125kg (the sign days 125 the written text 150kg) we would have been a combined weight between these two weights so decided to take seperate carts back. Noone said anything about it on route down, there were signs but too rushed to read them. Toilets on site were clean, parking easy. Pleasant stop, not overly expensive in terms of tourist price, the coaster was different but for us this stop was only a brief part of the day.

reviews.author: Alexandr Moskin
reviews.text: I had an unforgettable experience at Khu du lịch Thác Datanla in Da Lat! The natural beauty of the waterfalls and lush surroundings is truly refreshing, and the adventure elements like the alpine coaster added so much fun and excitement to the visit. It’s the perfect mix of peaceful nature and thrilling experiences — ideal for photos, memories, and a great day out exploring one of Vietnam’s most iconic attractions

reviews.author: Moses P
reviews.text: I love this zoo. There are many kind of animals! You can up close with tiger, lion, deer, iguana, and monkey!. The entry fee is only 60,000 VND for adult. Plenty of parking for motorbike. I would recommend to visit early morning before 9am or after 2pm. Overall, It is a must visit zoo in Vietnam!

reviews.author: Waiyin Cheng
reviews.text: I had such a fantastic time here! The staff were incredibly friendly and polite, which really made the experience enjoyable. The zipline and alpine kart rides were absolutely awesome and a definite highlight! 🤩🎢 While snacks were a bit pricey, everything else was truly great. 👍

reviews.author: Kelly Bosworth
reviews.text: The larger coaster gets very busy!!! We did the smaller one as the lines for the other side were insane!! The return trip on the alpine coaster is only an extra 20,000 so worth it if you just want to sit back and relax.

reviews.author: Ni
reviews.text: The waterfalls were beautiful but the highlight was the alpine coaster ride where certain sections can go really fast. The waterfalls can get really busy. We chose to walk to one of the waterfalls instead of the coaster, this walk was definitely quieter but a nicer peaceful experience.

reviews.author: Dak Mapson
reviews.text: Loved this waterfall and nice ride on the rollarcoaster to get there, was a great day trip from phan rang on the motorbike. The views and waterfall are very impressive. I arrived around 3pm and it wasn't so busy.

reviews.author: syamimie nawi
reviews.text: I visited Datanla during the rainy season, and despite the wet weather, it was a really enjoyable experience. The waterfall looks intimidating with the heavy flow, but the surrounding forest felt fresh and peaceful. Some areas were a bit slippery, so walking required extra caution, but the staff were helpful and guided visitors well. The alpine coaster was fun but slower due to the rain, which was understandable for safety. Overall, a great place to explore nature

reviews.author: Ikhwan Aiman
reviews.text: This is my first time going to Vietnam and the first place I want to try is Alpine coaster Datanla. The tickets is 240 000 dong per pax but it is really worth it. What I can describe is this is crazy fun. There are numerous lines of coaster and i took Alpine coaster 3 because it is new to public. I saw in the Titkok post that Alpines coaster 3 is longer and have more beautiful scenery compared to AC1. It was so much fun riding the coaster on the first track. I really recommend you this place if you come to Da Lat. How about you come here and taste the experience yourself? Enjoy your holiday!

reviews.author: Jonatan Jonsson
reviews.text: Great location for both viewing the waterfalls and riding the mountain coaster! There are long queues to the coaster, so go early in the day. If you like downhill adrenaline activities, also check out the Luge Cart (Mario Cart) experience also very close to Dalat. Its similar, but maybe even better for the action than this!

reviews.author: Chofifah Rahmatullaili
reviews.text: Yesterday i rode the alpine coaster and it was super fuuunnn! when we get to the waterfall area we can enjoy the waterfall or stop by the cafe. We also can buy documentation that taken while riding the alpine coaster.

reviews.author: Marcus Lee
reviews.text: My son and I purchased an adventurous package deal for 1 million and 50K VND/person (= to 40 USD), which included 1500 meters of alpine zip lining, a twisty roller coaster ride, and an admission to the upper and lower of the Datanla Water Falls. It was worthwhile and fun.

reviews.author: Simern Preet Kaur
reviews.text: we did canyoning, an amazing experience!! the alpine coaster was great too, easy to purchase at the counter and were offered to take photos that the cameras helped us capture during the ride. great memory to take away

reviews.author: Lara Makhrina
reviews.text: Heavy traffic of tourists, that’s only 1 minus. Nice waterfall, especially in February and March after rainy season. Alpine slides are amazing! Great experience!

reviews.author: tariq alabbasi
reviews.text: Great fun, worth the few hours will be there. Very kids friendly. Helpful staff. Most speak good English Weight restriction is 120kg for Zip Line.

reviews.author: Apinya Nookhayan
reviews.text: There should be a sign showing that for the Alpine coaster we should go to the Alpine number 3. Overall it was fun and sunny.

reviews.author: MA Reyes
reviews.text: This was my second time in Da Lat but my first visit to Datanla, and I truly enjoyed it! The scenery is relaxing, and the sound and view of the waterfalls were amazing. I also tried the alpine coaster, which was so much fun and made the experience even more memorable. There were quite a lot of tourists during my visit, but it was still very enjoyable overall. I recommend booking your tickets online in advance—it makes entry hassle-free and very convenient.

reviews.author: Dongik Choi
reviews.text: To get to the waterfall and back you need to ride the alpine coaster which cost 250k Dong per person. It is suitable and safe even for elderly. The waterfall is well maintained and have bathroom there. (They also have zipline at the waterfall)

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: What an adventure we had at Datanla Falls, its not only about the natural beauty but also the thrill of getting there. If you’re taking the Alpine Coaster, I recommend opting for the long track it’s worth it. You pass through beautiful forested slopes and end up right at one of the most scenic waterfalls in Da Lat. We had a blast on it and was so much fun. The views along the way are fantastic and the coaster adds a layer of excitement to the experience. There are two main falls to see the second one (reachable via a shorter coaster ride or walk) features a huge King Kong statue which makes for a fun and unexpected photo stop. From there, you’ll find a mix of scenic bridges, wooden walkways, and tropical gardens that offer fantastic photo angles. The first falls are more natural and powerful, with rock platforms and small viewing terraces perfect for photos or a short rest. There’s a clear path with steps surrounded by lush greenery, making the walk very enjoyable. Both sites are clean, accessible, and well maintained. You can easily spend a few hours here if you include both falls and the coaster ride. A great mix of nature and fun definitely worth adding to your Da Lat trip. Sunday 7:15 am–5 pm Monday 7:15 am–5 pm Tuesday 7:15 am–5 pm Wednesday 7:15 am–5 pm Thursday 7:15 am–5 pm Friday 7:15 am–5 pm Saturday 7:15 am–5 pm

reviews.author: Onnika van oosterbosch
reviews.text: Parking is 3000 VND per scooter. We did the toboggan #3 to waterfall #3 for 250 000 pp. According to the map (see photo) you can walk to the other waterfalls, unfortunately this path was closed. So instead we wanted to go to the other 2 waterfalls through the gate at the bottom, but they told us we needed to buy new tickets...so even though it is one park, you have to buy tickets 2ce to see all waterfalls. That was a bit of a disappointment as we think this wouldn't be the case if the path shown on the map was open. The toboggan route towards the waterfall was a lot of fun! The way back was short and less exciting.

reviews.author: Dale Martirez (Dayul)
reviews.text: Super fun experience today! It was relatively short but it felt so much longer overall! The coaster was really the highlight of the show! Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Akang
reviews.text: Very well developed with fun ride, plant/bridge/active deco s around this natural beauty of waterfalls, small but gorgeous.

reviews.author: Sarah Bartram
reviews.text: Really lovely waterfall. Used the alpine coaster to travel down and up. Once at the waterfall it was a great spot for pictures

reviews.author: Victor “Simply Vic” Sim
reviews.text: Beautiful waterfall layered with fun attractions (alpine coaster, ziplining, outdoor obstacles courses) definitely a memorable adventure for Da Lat visitors! Still operates under light rain. It was 250k vnd going for the longer alpine coaster (includes return from the waterfall stop) but also covers entry fee.

reviews.author: Rahul Bhatt
reviews.text: It is 7 km from Swiss-BelResort. Also need to wait in line about half hours beautiful place. All activities not available you have to check first. But it's worth it with waterfall.

reviews.author: Jimmy Lee
reviews.text: Suppose are good scenery and natural landscape but they allow people smoke around, feel sad for the great natural. For waterfall 1, roller-coaster can try down hill but not up hill.

reviews.author: Ngoc Huynh
reviews.text: Nice place not too far from the city center for a bit of trekking—lol. I visited Datanla Waterfall, and at the time, they were offering a Zipline + Coaster combo for 1 million VND per person. Not exactly cheap, but a fun experience overall. The zipline route is 1.5 km in total. The first stretch is 1 km, then you walk about 500 meters to the second zipline, which takes you to Waterfall 3. The coaster starts and ends at Waterfall 3 or Parking area. If you want to visit Waterfalls 1 and 2, you’ll need to hike uphill a bit. They’re not super close, but not too far either. After exploring, you can walk back to the Coaster at Waterfall 3. With the combo, you can use coaster to go up (parking area), then if you still want to use the coaster to go down, can exchange the, kind of, receipt to another coaster ticket for free (round trip). By the way, you can ride the coaster alone, which is a nice option if you prefer a solo experience. Be aware there’s another zipline and coaster option with a shorter distance which is cheaper and you may access from the main entrance. Staff are friendly. NOTE: if you want to actually see the waterfalls in their full beauty, don’t go during dry season - you will need a bit of rain for the best views (“water-fall”).

reviews.author: Oksana Oksana
reviews.text: A beautiful waterfall in Dalat. During the rainy season, it is powerful and especially beautiful. You can reach the waterfall on foot or by electric sled.

reviews.author: Xinyi Li
reviews.text: The cable car is definitely worth a try! it's a fun ride through the hill and the view is great. You can control the speed of the cart using the handle (pull up for brake and down for acceleration).

reviews.author: Shelley Williams
reviews.text: Couple of good waterfalls to visit on a rollercoaster style luge which you can control. Good fun but very confusing to understand where to get off and on. The ticket promises three goes but our ticket was cut up by the staff at the ticket barrier and didn’t seem helpful when we asked.
